comment: HELD-OUT should-cost benchmark set. These 14 fixtures were authored AFTER the engine calibration was frozen and are NEVER used for tuning — their sole purpose is to detect over-fitting to the primary set (benchmark/cost-fixtures.mjs). No part, material/process/volume/region combination is copied from the primary set. Reference anchors are engineering estimates derived from public teardown data and 2025 industry norms — they are NOT proprietary supplier quotes; each fixture's `source` states its basis honestly. Tolerance is a uniform ±35% band because estimate anchors cannot honestly support a tighter gate. Fixtures where the deterministic engine is EXPECTED to miss the band are kept deliberately (see their `note`) so the holdout retains discriminating power. Schema matches benchmark/cost-fixtures.mjs as consumed by benchmark/cost-run.mjs: { name, input:{material, process|route, weightKg, annualVolume, region}, refPriceEur, tol, source } plus holdout/id/note.

fixtures.name: Door module carrier, PA66-GF30 (injection moulded, >0.5 kg)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: glass-filled PA door carrier ~€5–7 at 250k/yr on a large-tonnage press, EU plant. Not a supplier quote.
fixtures.note: Task brief suggested PP-GF30; that grade is not in the engine material library, so the closest available glass-filled grade (PA66-GF30) is used.

fixtures.name: Washer-fluid bottle, PP (injection moulded, 1.4 kg)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: large polyolefin reservoir ~€3.5–5 at 180k/yr, EU-East. Not a supplier quote.
fixtures.note: Real washer bottles are usually blow-moulded HDPE; HDPE and blow moulding are not in the engine library, so the part is modelled as an injection-moulded PP reservoir of the same mass.

fixtures.name: Aluminium crash-management extrusion profile (cut to length)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: 6xxx structural extrusion ~€4.5–5.5/kg (billet + conversion + die), cut to length, EU, 120k/yr. Not a supplier quote.

fixtures.name: E-motor housing, HPDC Al + op-20 machining + wash (routed)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: finished machined HPDC e-motor housing ~€20–28 at 80k/yr EU (raw casting ~€13–15 + machining/wash). Not a supplier quote.

fixtures.name: Zinc die-cast exterior door-handle body
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: ZAMAK handle body ~€2–3 raw at 100k/yr, EU (excl. chrome plating). Not a supplier quote.

fixtures.name: Magnesium die-cast cross-car-beam bracket
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: Mg HPDC bracket ~€7–9/kg finished at 150k/yr, China. Not a supplier quote.
fixtures.note: The engine has no dedicated magnesium die-casting process; the HPDC (Aluminium) model explicitly lists the magnesium family as compatible and is used here.

fixtures.name: Large grey-iron sand casting, axle/diff housing (28 kg)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: grey-iron sand casting ~€1.1–1.5/kg raw+fettled at 30k/yr, India. Not a supplier quote.

fixtures.name: Laser-cut + bent steel bracket at LOW volume (10k/yr)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: job-shop laser+brake bracket ~€3.5–5.5 at 10k/yr, UK (setup-dominated; hard stamping tooling not viable at this volume). Not a supplier quote.

fixtures.name: CNC-machined Al fitting at HIGH volume (500k/yr) — expected engine miss
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: at 500k/yr real suppliers run rotary-transfer / multi-spindle cells with far shorter effective cycles, ~€4.5–6.5, China. Not a supplier quote.
fixtures.note: Deliberate discriminator: the engine's CNC model is a generic job-shop mill (fixed cycleBase + cyclePerKg, 2 setups) with no rotary-transfer or multi-spindle economy at extreme volume — expected to read HIGH vs this anchor.

fixtures.name: CFRP structural panel, RTM (1.8 kg) — expected engine miss
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: automotive RTM CFRP structural panel ~€80–100/kg piece price at 8k/yr, EU (fabric/preform waste, resin & consumables, NDT inspection). Not a supplier quote.
fixtures.note: Deliberate discriminator: the engine's composite model carries no preform cutting waste beyond utilisation, no resin/consumables line and no NDT/inspection burden — expected to read LOW vs this anchor.

fixtures.name: Titanium Ti-6Al-4V machined fitting (low volume) — expected engine miss
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: small machined Ti-6Al-4V fitting ~€45–70 at 5k/yr, US (Ti cutting speeds ~3–4x slower than Al/steel, heavy tool wear). Not a supplier quote.
fixtures.note: Deliberate discriminator: the engine's machining cycle model (cycleBase + cyclePerKg) is material-agnostic — it does not slow the cycle or raise perishable tooling for titanium's low machinability — expected to read LOW vs this anchor.

fixtures.name: Brass hydraulic manifold fitting (CNC milled)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: milled multi-port brass manifold fitting (not a simple screw-machine turning) ~€4–6 at 60k/yr, India; copper-alloy stock dominates the price. Not a supplier quote.

fixtures.name: Hydroformed HSS engine-cradle rail
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: tube-hydroformed HSS cradle rail ~€11–16 at 90k/yr, US (tube + pre-bend + hydroform + trim). Not a supplier quote.

fixtures.name: MIG-welded steel mount assembly (5.5 kg)
fixtures.source: Engineering estimate, 2025 industry norm: robotic MIG-welded mild-steel mount assembly ~€8–13 at 60k/yr, Mexico (child-part forming simplified into the weld-cell mass model). Not a supplier quote.
